questions.question: What is the difference between a compound and a mixture?
questions.correct_answer: A compound has chemical bonds between its constituent elements, whereas a mixture does not.
questions.explanation: Compounds have fixed ratios of elements and are formed through chemical reactions. Mixtures can be separated by physical means and do not exhibit new properties.

questions.question: What is the definition of an element?
questions.correct_answer: A substance consisting of only one type of atom.
questions.explanation: Elements are the simplest substances in chemistry and cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means.

questions.question: What is a homogeneous mixture?
questions.correct_answer: A mixture with uniform composition throughout.
questions.explanation: In a homogeneous mixture, the components are evenly distributed and cannot be distinguished from one another.

questions.question: What is the difference between atomic mass and atomic number?
questions.correct_answer: Atomic mass is the sum of protons and neutrons in an atom's nucleus, whereas atomic number is the number of protons in an atom's nucleus.
questions.explanation: Atomic mass is a measure of an element's relative abundance, while atomic number is unique to each element and determines its position in the periodic table.

questions.question: What is a compound formed by two or more elements?
questions.correct_answer: A chemical bond between the constituent elements forms a new substance with properties different from those of the individual elements.
questions.explanation: Compounds have fixed ratios of elements and are formed through chemical reactions, resulting in a new substance with unique properties.

questions.question: What is a mixture that exhibits the properties of its individual components?
questions.explanation: In a heterogeneous mixture, the components can be distinguished from one another and exhibit the properties of their individual components.

questions.question: What is the definition of a homogeneous solution?
questions.correct_answer: A solution in which the solute particles are evenly distributed throughout the solvent.
questions.explanation: In a homogeneous solution, the solute particles are dissolved and cannot be distinguished from one another.

questions.question: What is a mixture that has uniform composition throughout?
questions.explanation: In a homogeneous mixture, the components are evenly distributed and cannot be distinguished from one another.

questions.question: What is the difference between an element and a compound?
questions.correct_answer: An element has only one type of atom, whereas a compound has chemical bonds between its constituent elements.
questions.explanation: Elements are the simplest substances in chemistry and cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means. Compounds have fixed ratios of elements and are formed through chemical reactions.

questions.question: What is a compound that contains hydrogen?
questions.explanation: Organic compounds contain carbon-hydrogen bonds and can be found in living organisms.

notes.title: Definition of a Compound
notes.content: A compound is a substance formed by the chemical bonding of two or more elements. Compounds have fixed ratios of elements and are formed through chemical reactions, resulting in a new substance with unique properties.

notes.title: Elements
notes.content: Elements are the simplest substances in chemistry and consist of only one type of atom. They cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means.

notes.title: Homogeneous Mixtures
notes.content: A homogeneous mixture is a mixture with uniform composition throughout. In a homogeneous mixture, the components are evenly distributed and cannot be distinguished from one another.

notes.title: Atomic Mass vs Atomic Number
notes.content: Atomic mass is the sum of protons and neutrons in an atom's nucleus, whereas atomic number is the number of protons in an atom's nucleus. Atomic mass is a measure of an element's relative abundance, while atomic number is unique to each element and determines its position in the periodic table.

notes.title: Compounds
notes.content: A compound is a substance formed by the chemical bonding of two or more elements. Compounds have fixed ratios of elements and are formed through chemical reactions, resulting in a new substance with unique properties.

notes.title: Heterogeneous Mixtures
notes.content: A heterogeneous mixture is a mixture that exhibits the properties of its individual components. In a heterogeneous mixture, the components can be distinguished from one another and exhibit the properties of their individual components.

notes.title: Homogeneous Solutions
notes.content: A homogeneous solution is a solution in which the solute particles are evenly distributed throughout the solvent. In a homogeneous solution, the solute particles are dissolved and cannot be distinguished from one another.

notes.title: Homogeneous Mixtures
notes.content: A homogeneous mixture is a mixture with uniform composition throughout. In a homogeneous mixture, the components are evenly distributed and cannot be distinguished from one another.

notes.title: Elements vs Compounds
notes.content: An element has only one type of atom, whereas a compound has chemical bonds between its constituent elements. Elements are the simplest substances in chemistry and cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means. Compounds have fixed ratios of elements and are formed through chemical reactions.

notes.title: Organic Compounds
notes.content: An organic compound is a compound that contains hydrogen. Organic compounds can be found in living organisms and contain carbon-hydrogen bonds.

facts.fact: The smallest unit of matter that retains the properties of an element is an atom.
The most abundant element in the universe is hydrogen.
A mixture can be separated into its individual components by physical means, such as filtration or distillation.
The atomic mass of an element is the sum of protons and neutrons in an atom's nucleus.
A compound has unique properties that are different from those of its individual components.
Heterogeneous mixtures exhibit the properties of their individual components.
Homogeneous solutions have uniform composition throughout.
Elements are the simplest substances in chemistry and consist of only one type of atom.
Compounds are formed through chemical reactions and have fixed ratios of elements.
Organic compounds contain carbon-hydrogen bonds and can be found in living organisms.
